headline: Is PayPal a Buy Ahead of Business Restructuring After 45% Selloff?
summary: PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) currently trades around $50.48, while the average Wall Street price target sits at $52.97. That leaves an implied upside of roughly 4.9%, which is a remarkably narrow gap for a stock that has been through this much turbulence. PayPal operates one of the largest digital payments platforms, spanning branded checkout, Venmo, and global ... Is PayPal a Buy Ahead of Business Restructuring After 45% Selloff?

headline: This Week In Digital Payment - Glass Partners With Mastercard To Revolutionize Government Transactions
summary: Glass, a GovTech company, has entered Mastercard Start Path’s inaugural Corporate Solutions program to enhance digital payment infrastructure for the public sector. This collaboration aims to modernize government transactions by providing scalable solutions that improve efficiency, transparency, and intelligence in public finance. As part of a select group of fintech companies, Glass gains access to Mastercard’s extensive network, fostering innovation and opportunities within the global...
